title: Customer Interaction
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct respectful sentences (Sonkeigo) and highly formal apologies/acknowledgments used in customer service and business.
instructions: In this roleplay, you are a staff member speaking to a very important customer. Reply verbally in Japanese based on the English prompts, using highly respectful language.

contentItems.text: Remember: You must use Sonkeigo for the customer's actions.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) Welcome. 2) What will you eat? 3) I am very sorry. 4) Understood.
scenario: A waiter speaking to a VIP customer.

subTasks.prompt: Ask for their order respectfully. Say: 'What will you eat?'

sampleResponse.targetText: なにを めしあがりますか。

subTasks.prompt: The kitchen is out of an item. Apologize formally. Say: 'I am very sorry.'

sampleResponse.targetText: もうしわけございません。

subTasks.prompt: Acknowledge their new order formally. Say: 'Understood.'

scenarioContext.targetText: すしを おねがいします。

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: なにを めしあがりますか。
もうしわけございません。

requiredKeywords: もうしわけございません
acceptableParaphrases: Accept variations in tone/pitch, but the words must be exact for formal business Keigo.

criteria.name: Sonkeigo Usage
criteria.description: Did the learner accurately say 'meshiagarimasu ka' instead of 'tabemasu ka'?

criteria.name: Formal Apology
criteria.description: Did the learner say 'moushiwake gozaimasen' instead of 'gomen nasai'?

criteria.name: Formal Acknowledgment
criteria.description: Did the learner say 'kashikomarimashita' instead of 'wakarimashita'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: いらっしゃいませ。なにを めしあがりますか。もうしわけございません。かしこまりました。

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic speaking! You sound highly professional and polite.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the set phrases. 'Gomen nasai' is completely unacceptable in customer service; you must use 'Moushiwake gozaimasen'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you use 'Kashikomarimashita' for 'Understood'. 'Wakarimashita' is too casual for a VIP.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: いらっしゃいませ。なにを めしあがりますか。もうしわけございません。かしこまりました。

hintText: Do not use 'Tabemasu' or 'Wakarimashita'. Use their highly formal equivalents.
